items.title: Anthropic "pauses" token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK
items.summary: Move originally planned for Monday would have heavily increased power users' costs.

items.title: Exclusive eBook: How AI is becoming the next military advisor
items.summary: A collection of stories about how militaries are using AI models to make decisions. This subscriber-only eBook is a package of six stories that were originally published in MIT Technology Review between April 11, 2025, and April 21, 2026, and have been updated to reflect recent developments. by Jame

items.title: SpaceX valuation balloons to $2.6T, briefly passes Amazon
items.summary: SpaceX's valuation has increased by $1 trillion since its shares started trading on Friday.

items.title: Pentagon boasts of using AI to write reports mandated by Congress
items.summary: Pentagon also claims 1.5 million personnel are using generative AI tools.

items.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
items.summary: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7, introducing new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google’s latest AI models to its devices.

items.title: Apple 2027 rumors: AirPods with cameras for AI and the second folding iPhone
items.summary: Now that we're clear of WWDC and all of the new AI-powered features coming to Apple's platforms, Bloomberg reporter Mark Gurman has more details about rumored new hardware, like the camera-equipped AirPods he'd previously written about. He says they are currently on schedule for a late 2027 launch,

items.title: Qualcomm’s latest chip hints that more powerful smart glasses could be on the way
items.summary: Smart glasses are still a nascent category, but chipmaker Qualcomm is hard at work upgrading the silicon to power the next wave of XR devices: the Snapdragon Reality Elite. Although Qualcomm is announcing the chip today at Augmented World Expo, we've technically already gotten a hands-on with a devi

items.title: Sixty percent of US consumers say ‘AI’ in brand messaging is a turnoff, survey finds
items.summary: WordPress VIP’s latest survey suggests consumers are wary of AI-generated answers even as companies increasingly view AI search as an important referral channel.

items.title: SpaceX to acquire AI coding platform Cursor for $60 billion
items.summary: Separately, neither could compete. Now they hope they can.

items.title: HPE AI Factory With NVIDIA Expands for the Era of Agents
items.summary: Enterprises are moving agentic AI from proof of concept to production — and the next generation of AI factories are built for the era of agents. items.title: Leaked financial docs show OpenAI is losing billions of dollars a year
items.summary: Audited accounting shows growing revenues being dwarfed by R&D, other expenses.

items.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
items.summary: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

items.title: DOJ claims xAI’s unpermitted gas turbines are a matter of ‘national, economic, and energy security’
items.summary: The Justice department says the Pentagon needs xAI to keep using its unpermitted gas turbines.

items.title: Fastest, Largest, Strongest: NVIDIA Blackwell Sweeps MLPerf Training 6.0
items.summary: Every breakthrough AI model starts the same way: with a training run. items.title: Plaud says its software business topped $100M in ARR after shipping over 2M AI notetakers
items.summary: Plaud is trying to make a mark in a crowded market full of AI-powered meeting notetakers.

items.title: Robinhood’s note on 10% layoffs shows blaming AI isn’t cutting it
items.summary: Unlike many of his tech industry peers who have cut thousands of jobs citing the need to restructure to make the most of AI, Robinhood's CEO Vlad Tenev conspicuously made no mention of AI in his note about layoffs.

items.title: Probably raises $9M to build a more reliable kind of AI
items.summary: Probably wants to prevent hallucinations and factual errors from reaching users, and achieve accuracy on par with deterministic systems.

items.title: The Download: the first brain implant power user and South Korea’s AI obsession
items.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. This man with ALS is the first “power user” of a brain implant that lets him speak Casey Harrell has had a set of electrodes embedded in his brain for

items.title: SpaceX is officially buying Cursor for $60 billion
items.summary: Days after its massive IPO, SpaceX says it is spending $60 billion to buy Cursor - a bet designed to help Elon Musk's sprawling rocket / AI / social media behemoth win over lucrative enterprise customers and close the gap with AI rivals like Anthropic and OpenAI. items.title: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
items.summary: The deal is supposed to help SpaceX's struggling AI division. The company told IPO investors it sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI.

items.title: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to steal 2FA code from users
items.summary: SearchLeak exploit shows why the industry's approach to LLM security fails over and over.

items.title: ChatGPT’s market share slips below 50% for first time
items.summary: The chatbot still remains the most popular AI assistant worldwide with over 1.1 billion monthly users, followed by Gemini with 662 million and Claude with 245 million.

items.title: Want to get a data center online quickly? Give it some flex.
items.summary: At the end of a tense and scoreless first half of a soccer match between the English men’s team and rival Germany, millions of Brits let out a collective sigh and did what they so often do in moments of stress: They made tea. items.title: Malaysia’s AI agent-powered messaging app Respond.io raises $62.5M, eyes acquisitions
items.summary: Respond.io, one of Malaysia's startups to watch, uses AI agents to handle high volumes of customer inquiries and charges per convo, not per seat.

items.summary: arXiv:2606.14777v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many moments in the real world do not wait for a user to ask. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14778v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-term action anticipation (LTA) aims to predict an ordered sequence of future verb-noun actions from a partially observed video. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14780v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Clickbait content on video-sharing platforms poses a significant challenge to information reliability, yet progress in automated detection has been constrained by the lack of large-scale, high-quality multimodal datasets. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14781v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Underwater imaging plays a crucial role in ocean engineering, although captured data often suffer from poor visibility and color distortion. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14782v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong vision-language reasoning, but long visual contexts enlarge the KV cache and increase decoding latency. items.title: The Vision Encoder as a Privacy Boundary: Visual-Token Side Channels in Encoder-Free Vision-Language Models
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14783v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A vision encoder compresses image pixels into semantic embeddings, implicitly acting as a privacy boundary by preserving semantic content while attenuating pixel-local detail required for exact text recovery. items.title: Inside the fight over Claude Mythos 5
items.summary: As the rest of the country celebrated the USA's first World Cup win and the New York Knicks championship, Anthropic spent its weekend fighting the Trump administration over its latest model release. items.title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
items.summary: OpenAI introduces Deployment Simulation, a method to predict AI model behavior before deployment using real conversation data to improve safety and evaluation accuracy.

items.title: Sundar Pichai faces boos, walkout at Stanford graduation ceremony over Google’s Israel, ICE ties
items.summary: AI is once again at the heart of a college graduation protest — this time for the technology's use in Google's defense contracts.

items.title: The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak
items.summary: The Trump administration's decision that forced Anthropic to pull its latest cybersecurity models could be reactionary, retaliatory, or both, but the message is clear: The AI industry isn't immune from U.S. government interference.

items.title: Facebook’s new AI Mode search gets its info from public posts
items.summary: Your public Facebook posts could help inform AI-generated results in Meta's new AI Mode. When you search on Facebook, the "AI Mode" option will appear alongside the usual search modes like "People" and "Marketplace." items.summary: Debt sale set to test investor appetite for further exposure to AI sector amid a deluge of borrowing.

items.title: Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
items.summary: Meta announced Monday that it's rolling out a wave of new AI features on Facebook, the latest sign of the company's effort to catch up in the AI race and keep users more engaged on the platform.

items.title: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
items.summary: At Washington's request, Anthropic suddenly took its newest and most powerful AI models offline over the weekend. items.title: Big Tech’s desperate last push at AI regulation
items.summary: For months, Big Tech's Washington lobbyists have chased after the holy grail of pro-AI legislation: preemption. items.title: Cybersecurity vets protest ‘dangerous’ US government ban on Anthropic’s most powerful models
items.summary: A group made up of dozens of cybersecurity experts urged the White House to remove export-control restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable and Mythos models, arguing that the order is going to limit the ability of cybersecurity defenders to secure their software and products.

items.title: This man with ALS is “the first power user” of a brain implant that lets him speak
items.summary: Casey Harrell has had a set of electrodes embedded in his brain for almost three years. Harrell, who has amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) and is paralyzed, first used his brain-computer interface (BCI) to “speak” sentences with the help of a research team in 2023. items.title: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6B
items.summary: Salesforce says it wants to use Fin's team and technology to improve Agentforce, its existing enterprise platform that businesses can use to build custom AI agents that automate tasks.

items.summary: Today, I’m talking with Adam Bry, who is CEO of Skydio, the leading US maker of autonomous drones. items.title: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech
items.summary: Indian IT services company HCLTech is investing $150 million in the Bengaluru startup.

items.title: As AI agents become employees, NewCore emerges with $66M to give them identities
items.summary: NewCore argues the next challenge in enterprise security will be managing AI agents, not people.

items.title: A satellite just learned to find things on its own — here’s what that means
items.summary: In April, for the first time ever, an Earth observation satellite found what it was looking for, all on its own.

items.title: These new solid-state ACs promise a cool future. Scientists aren’t so sure.
items.summary: After three years of record-­breaking heat, this one is set to be yet another scorcher. Air-conditioning? Not going anywhere. items.title: The AI layoff wave is becoming a powder keg
items.summary: At the very moment that tens of thousands of workers are being shown the door, a small cohort of AI insiders is becoming wealthy on a scale that's hard to comprehend.

items.title: China may have accessed Mythos
items.summary: According to a new report from Semafor, the White House's decision to impose export restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos was driven in part by fears that it had been accessed by a group linked to China. items.title: Introducing the OpenAI Partner Network
items.summary: OpenAI launches the Partner Network, investing $150M to help global partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation.
